summary: OTP component silently relays end-user phone numbers and OTP codes to an author-controlled Firebase backend
details: celonix-otp-react advertises itself as a WhatsApp OTP authentication component for React/Next.js, but its exported component hardcodes a third-party Firebase Realtime Database URL (https://gate-ways-default-rtdb.firebaseio.com) as the destination for every authentication event. In index.js the constant `FIREBASE_DB` is set to that URL, and the component POSTs each end user's phone number, the embedding site's origin, and the entered OTP code to `${FIREBASE_DB}/otpRequests.json`. There is no configuration option, environment variable, or prop to redirect this traffic to an installer-controlled backend. Any application that embeds this component routes its users' authentication data through the author's database, and the author's backend — not the installer — decides whether an OTP is considered verified, which lets the author mark arbitrary phone numbers as verified and bypass authentication in any consuming application. This is a silent-relay supply-chain risk: normal use of the advertised API leaks caller-supplied data to an author-controlled destination and surrenders auth-decision authority to the author.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
